title: Delicate Japanese Kabocha Agedashi
description: A traditional Japanese dish featuring deep-fried kabocha squash in a light broth, served in a lacquerware bowl. This earthy and chewy crowd-pleaser is egg-free and perfect for any occasion.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a deep frying pan to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a shallow dish, mix together the flour and cornstarch.
Toss the kabocha squash cubes in the flour mixture to coat.
Carefully add the coated squash cubes to the hot oil and deep-fry until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes.
Remove the fried squash from the oil with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
In a small saucepan, combine the dashi broth, soy sauce, and mirin, and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Add the grated ginger to the broth and cook for 1 minute.
To serve, place a few pieces of the fried squash in a lacquerware bowl and ladle the hot broth over the top.
